# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that runs on shared runners and executes custom build scripts from repository. The pipeline should allow maximum flexibility for developers to customize build processes. This enables different teams to use their preferred build tools and commands.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - deploy
  
before_script:
- apt-get update --quiet -qq && echo "deb [arch=amd64] http://ftp.de.debian.org/debian jessie main" > /etc/apt/sources.list.d/jessie.list 
# install dependencies for custom build tools that the developer may use in their CI job (e.g., make, cmake...)  
- apt-get -qq update && apt-get install --quiet -y git nodejs python2 ruby-dev gcc make libpq-dev unixODBC++ devscripts 
# clone repository and navigate to project directory on shared runner (e.g., $CI_PROJECT_DIR)  
script: echo "$TRAVIS" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/*.* -R .git; cd customBuildJobs ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script  (e.g., make, cmake...)
# ... here define as many build scripts or commands developers can specify for their project...   and then repeat this process with them using a variable like $CI_PROJECT_*.* that GitLab CI will pass to your jobs file upon triggering the pipeline event.. e  echo "$MYCUSTOMJOBSCRIPTS" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/*.sh -R .git; cd customBuildTasks ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run build scripts defined in travis script ...
  
# use the same variable for your test and deploy stages of CI pipeline, e  echo "$CI_COMMIT_REF_NAME" > /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/*.sh -R .git; cd customBuildJobs ;./run/myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script ...
  
# use git commands (e.g., git clone) as you see fit, e  echo "$GIT_CLONE" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/* -R .git; cd customBuildJobs ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script ...
  
# once done, save and share your test results for further inspection.  Also specify which file(s) you want to include or exclude from sharing so they remain private unless otherwise specified... e echo "$SHARE_WITH" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/* -R .git; cd customBuildJobs ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script ...
  
deploy: 
# use git commands here (e.g., echo "$GIT_COMMIT" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/* -R .git; cd customBuildTasks ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script ...
  
# after successfully testing, push changes back to your repository. You can do this either with a git command or as part of test/deploy stage if required... e  echo "$PUSH_TO_REPO" >> /tmp/test1 && chmod +x ./custombuildjobs/.travis/* -R .git; cd customBuildJobs ;./run-myCustomJobScripts # run the job defined in travis script ...
